root_text.sn35.150:1.3: katamā ca sā, bhikkhave, nibbānasappāyā paṭipadā?
root_text.sn35.150:3.1: “Yaṁ panāniccaṁ dukkhaṁ vā taṁ sukhaṁ vā”ti?
root_text.sn35.150:5.1: “Yaṁ panāniccaṁ dukkhaṁ vipariṇāmadhammaṁ, kallaṁ nu taṁ samanupassituṁ:
root_text.sn35.150:9.3: yampidaṁ manosamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tampi niccaṁ vā aniccaṁ vā”ti?
root_text.sn35.150:11.1: “Yaṁ panāniccaṁ dukkhaṁ vā taṁ sukhaṁ vā”ti?
root_text.sn35.150:13.1: “Yaṁ panāniccaṁ dukkhaṁ vipariṇāmadhammaṁ, kallaṁ nu taṁ samanupassituṁ:
root_text.sn35.150:15.1: “Evaṁ passaṁ, bhikkhave, sutavā ariyasāvako cakkhusmimpi nibbindati, rūpesupi nibbindati, cakkhuviññāṇepi nibbindati, cakkhusamphassepi nibbindati …pe…
root_text.sn35.150:15.2: yampidaṁ manosamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tasmimpi nibbindati.
root_text.sn35.150:15.5: Ayaṁ kho sā, bhikkhave, nibbānasappāyā paṭipadā”ti.

translation_text.sn35.150:1.1: “Mendicants, I will teach you a practice that’s conducive to extinguishment.
translation_text.sn35.150:1.3: And what is that practice that’s conducive to extinguishment?
translation_text.sn35.150:1.5: Is the eye permanent or impermanent?”
translation_text.sn35.150:3.1: “But if it’s impermanent, is it suffering or happiness?”
translation_text.sn35.150:5.1: “But if it’s impermanent, suffering, and perishable, is it fit to be regarded thus:
translation_text.sn35.150:5.2: ‘This is mine, I am this, this is my self’?”
translation_text.sn35.150:9.3: The pleasant, painful, or neutral feeling that arises dependent on mind contact: is that permanent or impermanent?”
translation_text.sn35.150:11.1: “But if it’s impermanent, is it suffering or happiness?”
translation_text.sn35.150:13.1: “But if it’s impermanent, suffering, and perishable, is it fit to be regarded thus:
translation_text.sn35.150:13.2: ‘This is mine, I am this, this is my self’?”
translation_text.sn35.150:15.1: “Seeing this, a learned noble disciple grows disillusioned with the eye, sights, eye consciousness, and eye contact. And they grow disillusioned with the painful, pleasant, or neutral feeling that arises dependent on eye contact.
translation_text.sn35.150:15.2: They grow disillusioned with the ear … nose … tongue … body … mind … painful, pleasant, or neutral feeling that arises dependent on mind contact.
translation_text.sn35.150:15.3: Being disillusioned, desire fades away. When desire fades away they’re freed. …
translation_text.sn35.150:15.4: They understand: ‘… there is nothing further for this place.’
translation_text.sn35.150:15.5: This is that practice that’s conducive to extinguishment.”
